diagrams.title: OSI 7-Layer Model
diagrams.description: Shows the seven layers of OSI model with their numbers and names in order from bottom to top
elements: Arrows showing upward and downward data flow
diagrams.purpose: Understanding layer hierarchy and PDU types at each level

diagrams.title: TCP/IP vs OSI Model Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing how TCP/IP 4 layers map to OSI 7 layers
elements: TCP/IP Application = OSI Session + Presentation + Application
TCP/IP Network Access = OSI Data Link + Physical
diagrams.purpose: Understanding relationship between theoretical and practical models

diagrams.title: Encapsulation Process
diagrams.description: Shows how data gets wrapped with headers at each layer during transmission
elements: Application Data → Transport Segment (adds port header) → Network Packet (adds IP header) → Data Link Frame (adds MAC header + trailer) → Physical Bits
Arrow showing downward movement through layers with growing PDU
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how data is transformed and header information added

diagrams.title: Decapsulation Process
diagrams.description: Shows reverse process where headers are removed as data moves up
elements: Physical Bits → Data Link Frame (removes MAC) → Network Packet (removes IP) → Transport Segment (removes port) → Application Data
Arrow showing upward movement through layers with shrinking PDU
diagrams.purpose: Understanding data extraction process at receiving end
